title: Currant rock cakes
subtitle: Crusty rough-topped scones with dried fruit
excerpt: Currant rock cakes are spooned into rough mounds rather than cut, giving the crusty, rocky top the name. Self-raising flour, butter, sugar and dried fruit; ready from the bowl in under thirty minutes.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: rock cakes appear under tea-table cakes as a child's bake. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): the Yorkshire 'fat rascal' is documented as a regional variant with cherries and almonds on top. Mrs Rundell, A New System of Domestic Cookery (1806), Project Gutenberg: an early form of the spooned-fruit bun appears as 'rock cakes' in the household-baking section.

recipe.freezeNotes: Bake, cool, freeze in a bag for up to 3 months. Warm in a 160°C oven for 5 minutes to refresh.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Rub the butter into the flour and chill the dry mix; add the liquid and fruit on the morning.

content.text: Rock cakes are the simplest fruit bake on the tea table: a stiff scone-style mix with currants and sultanas, spooned onto a tray in rough mounds rather than cut. The rocky top is the point — no flour, no rolling, no cutter; just a forkful of dough dropped onto the tray.
Best on the day they are baked; on day two they dry out quickly. Warm in a low oven for five minutes to refresh.

items.prepNote: approximately, to bring the dough together

content.text: Rub in, mix in the fruit

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Sift the {{self-raising-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{mixed-spice}} into a bowl. Rub in the {{unsalted-butter}} until the mix looks like coarse breadcrumbs. Stir in the {{caster-sugar}}, {{dried-currants}} and {{sultanas}}.

Beat the {{eggs}} with the {{whole-milk}} and pour into the bowl. Stir with a fork until the dough comes together in a stiff, shaggy mass; it should hold its shape when piled on the fork. Add another tablespoon of milk if it is too dry. Drop twelve rough mounds onto a lined baking tray, leaving 3 cm between each. Scatter the {{demerara-sugar}} over the tops.
Bake for 14–16 minutes; the tops should be deep gold and the rocky peaks should look crisp. Cool on a rack 5 minutes before eating; eat the same day for the best texture.
